2026-04-20T11:09:02.5673368Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-20T11:09:02.5674440Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-20T11:09:02.5674875Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-20T11:09:02.5675242Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-20T11:09:02.5675552Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-20T11:09:02.5675791Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-20T11:09:02.5675956Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-20T11:09:02.5676146Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-20T11:09:02.5676325Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-20T11:09:02.5676558Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-04-20T11:09:02.5676772Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-20T11:09:02.5677064Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-20T11:09:02.5677327Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

2026-04-20T11:11:31.0749846Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-20T11:10:08.257807916Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58381,\"title\":\"[Storage] Docs: Document snapshot and version transfer support in Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Blobs README\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Blobs`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/storage/Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Blobs/`\\n**Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (PR #57368) by `@amnguye`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #57368 added two new properties to `BlobStorageResourceOptions`:\\n\\n- `Snapshot` — specifies a blob snapshot identifier to use as the source of a transfer (copy, download, or pause/resume)\\n- `VersionId` — specifies a blob version identifier to use as the source of a transfer (copy, download, or pause/resume)\\n\\nThese are mutually exclusive: setting both throws an exception. The CHANGELOG was updated, but the README and samples were not.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The README `Examples` section has no example showing how to transfer a specific blob snapshot using `BlobStorageResourceOptions.Snapshot`\\n- The README `Examples` section has no example showing how to transfer a specific blob version using `BlobStorageResourceOptions.VersionId`\\n- The samples file `sdk/storage/Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Blobs/samples/Sample01b_HelloWorldAsync.cs` has no test-backed snippet (`#region Snippet:`) for either feature\\n- The new `Snapshot` and `VersionId` properties are not mentioned anywhere in the README\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\nNo direct edits to `README.md` are needed for the code examples themselves — the snippet tooling injects code automatically. However, the README does need a new section heading and snippet placeholder added (Step 2).\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\n#### 2a. Add snippet methods to the existing sample file\\n\\n**File to edit:** `sdk/storage/Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Blobs/samples/Sample01b_HelloWorldAsync.cs`\\n\\nAdd two new `[Test]` methods to the existing sample class. Insert them after the `BlobCopy` section (near `s2sCopyBlob` / `s2sCopyBlobContainer` snippets):\\n\\n```csharp\\n[Test]\\n[LiveOnly]\\n[NonParallelizable]\\npublic async Task DownloadBlobSnapshotAsync()\\n{\\n    // Create a blob and a snapshot for demonstration\\n#if !SNIPPET\\n    string connectionString = StorageConnectionString;\\n    string containerName = Randomize(\\u0026#34;sample-container\\u0026#34;);\\n    string blobName = Randomize(\\u0026#34;sample-blob\\u0026#34;);\\n    string downloadPath = Path.Combine(Path.GetTempPath(), Randomize(\\u0026#34;downloaded\\u0026#34;));\\n    BlobServiceClient service = new BlobServiceClient(connectionString);\\n    BlobContainerClient container = service.GetBlobContainerClient(containerName);\\n    await container.CreateIfNotExistsAsync();\\n    BlockBlobClient blob = container.GetBlockBlobClient(blobName);\\n    await blob.UploadAsync(BinaryData.FromString(\\u0026#34;hello snapshot\\u0026#34;));\\n    BlobSnapshotInfo snapshotInfo = await blob.CreateSnapshotAsync();\\n    string snapshotId = snapshotInfo.Snapshot;\\n    Uri blobUri = blob.Uri;\\n    TokenCredential tokenCredential = new DefaultAzureCredential();\\n    try\\n    {\\n#endif\\n    #region Snippet:DownloadBlobSnapshot\\n    BlobsStorageResourceProvider blobs = new(tokenCredential);\\n    TransferManager transferManager = new TransferManager();\\n\\n    BlockBlobStorageResourceOptions sourceOptions = new BlockBlobStorageResourceOptions()\\n    {\\n        Snapshot = snapshotId\\n    };\\n\\n    TransferOperation transferOperation = await transferManager.StartTransferAsync(\\n        sourceResource: await blobs.FromBlobAsync(blobUri, sourceOptions),\\n        destinationResource: LocalFilesStorageResourceProvider.FromFile(downloadPath));\\n    await transferOperation.WaitForCompletionAsync();\\n    #endregion\\n#if !SNIPPET\\n    }\\n    finally\\n    {\\n        await container.DeleteIfExistsAsync();\\n        if (File.Exists(downloadPath)) File.Delete(downloadPath);\\n    }\\n#endif\\n}\\n\\n[Test]\\n[LiveOnly]\\n[NonParallelizable]\\npublic async Task CopyBlobVersionAsync()\\n{\\n#if !SNIPPET\\n    string connectionString = StorageConnectionString;\\n    string containerName = Randomize(\\u0026#34;sample-container\\u0026#34;);\\n    string srcBlobName = Randomize(\\u0026#34;sample-blob\\u0026#34;);\\n    string dstBlobName = Randomize(\\u0026#34;sample-blob-dest\\u0026#34;);\\n    BlobServiceClient service = new BlobServiceClient(connectionString);\\n    BlobContainerClient container = service.GetBlobContainerClient(containerName);\\n    await container.CreateIfNotExistsAsync();\\n    BlockBlobClient srcBlob = container.GetBlockBlobClient(srcBlobName);\\n    await srcBlob.UploadAsync(BinaryData.FromString(\\u0026#34;version content\\u0026#34;));\\n    // Upload again to create a new version; capture the first version ID from properties\\n    BlobProperties props = await srcBlob.GetPropertiesAsync();\\n    string versionId = props.VersionId;\\n    Uri srcBlobUri = srcBlob.Uri;\\n    Uri dstBlobUri = container.GetBlockBlobClient(dstBlobName).Uri;\\n    TokenCredential tokenCredential = new DefaultAzureCredential();\\n    try\\n    {\\n#endif\\n    #region Snippet:CopyBlobVersion\\n    BlobsStorageResourceProvider blobs = new(tokenCredential);\\n    TransferManager transferManager = new TransferManager();\\n\\n    BlockBlobStorageResourceOptions sourceOptions = new BlockBlobStorageResourceOptions()\\n    {\\n        VersionId = versionId\\n    };\\n\\n    TransferOperation transferOperation = await transferManager.StartTransferAsync(\\n        sourceResource: await blobs.FromBlobAsync(srcBlobUri, sourceOptions),\\n        destinationResource: await blobs.FromBlobAsync(dstBlobUri, new BlockBlobStorageResourceOptions()));\\n    await transferOperation.WaitForCompletionAsync();\\n    #endregion\\n#if !SNIPPET\\n    }\\n    finally\\n    {\\n        await container.DeleteIfExistsAsync();\\n    }\\n#endif\\n}\\n```\\n\\n#### 2b. Add snippet placeholders to the README\\n\\n**File to edit:** `sdk/storage/Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Blobs/README.md`\\n\\nAdd the following section after the existing `### Blob Copy` section (before `### Resume using ShareFilesStorageResourceProvider`):\\n\\n````markdown\\n### Snapshot and Version Transfers\\n\\nThe `Snapshot` and `VersionId` properties on `BlobStorageResourceOptions` allow targeting a specific point-in-time copy of a blob as the transfer source. These options are only valid for the source resource; they cannot be combined.\\n\\nDownload a specific blob snapshot:\\n\\n```C# Snippet:DownloadBlobSnapshot\\n```\\n\\nCopy a specific blob version to a new blob:\\n\\n```C# Snippet:CopyBlobVersion\\n```\\n````\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README already contains all required sections:\\n1. ✅ Getting started (Install the package, Prerequisites, Authenticate the client)\\n2. ✅ Key concepts\\n3. ✅ Examples — **needs the new Snapshot and Version subsections added per Step 2b**\\n4. ✅ Troubleshooting\\n5. ✅ Next steps\\n6. ✅ Contributing\\n\\nNo structural changes needed beyond the new subsection in Examples.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/storage/Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Blobs/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/storage/Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Blobs/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 storage` — injects the new `#region Snippet:` code into the README placeholders; verify the git diff shows the README was updated\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 storage` — no new public types were added, but run to confirm\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/storage/Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Blobs/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; - `pkgs.dev.azure.com`\\n\\u0026gt; - `releaseassets.githubusercontent.com`\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; ```yaml\\n\\u0026gt; network:\\n\\u0026gt;   allowed:\\n\\u0026gt;     - defaults\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;pkgs.dev.azure.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;releaseassets.githubusercontent.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt; - `pkgs.dev.azure.com`\\n\\u0026gt; - `releaseassets.githubusercontent.com`\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; 2026-04-20T11:11:31.0901110Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-20T11:10:20.402280469Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58381,\"title\":\"[Storage] Docs: Document snapshot and version transfer support in Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Blobs README\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Blobs`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/storage/Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Blobs/`\\n**Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (PR #57368) by `@amnguye`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #57368 added two new properties to `BlobStorageResourceOptions`:\\n\\n- `Snapshot` — specifies a blob snapshot identifier to use as the source of a transfer (copy, download, or pause/resume)\\n- `VersionId` — specifies a blob version identifier to use as the source of a transfer (copy, download, or pause/resume)\\n\\nThese are mutually exclusive: setting both throws an exception. The CHANGELOG was updated, but the README and samples were not.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The README `Examples` section has no example showing how to transfer a specific blob snapshot using `BlobStorageResourceOptions.Snapshot`\\n- The README `Examples` section has no example showing how to transfer a specific blob version using `BlobStorageResourceOptions.VersionId`\\n- The samples file `sdk/storage/Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Blobs/samples/Sample01b_HelloWorldAsync.cs` has no test-backed snippet (`#region Snippet:`) for either feature\\n- The new `Snapshot` and `VersionId` properties are not mentioned anywhere in the README\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\nNo direct edits to `README.md` are needed for the code examples themselves — the snippet tooling injects code automatically. However, the README does need a new section heading and snippet placeholder added (Step 2).\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\n#### 2a. Add snippet methods to the existing sample file\\n\\n**File to edit:** `sdk/storage/Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Blobs/samples/Sample01b_HelloWorldAsync.cs`\\n\\nAdd two new `[Test]` methods to the existing sample class. Insert them after the `BlobCopy` section (near `s2sCopyBlob` / `s2sCopyBlobContainer` snippets):\\n\\n```csharp\\n[Test]\\n[LiveOnly]\\n[NonParallelizable]\\npublic async Task DownloadBlobSnapshotAsync()\\n{\\n    // Create a blob and a snapshot for demonstration\\n#if !SNIPPET\\n    string connectionString = StorageConnectionString;\\n    string containerName = Randomize(\\u0026#34;sample-container\\u0026#34;);\\n    string blobName = Randomize(\\u0026#34;sample-blob\\u0026#34;);\\n    string downloadPath = Path.Combine(Path.GetTempPath(), Randomize(\\u0026#34;downloaded\\u0026#34;));\\n    BlobServiceClient service = new BlobServiceClient(connectionString);\\n    BlobContainerClient container = service.GetBlobContainerClient(containerName);\\n    await container.CreateIfNotExistsAsync();\\n    BlockBlobClient blob = container.GetBlockBlobClient(blobName);\\n    await blob.UploadAsync(BinaryData.FromString(\\u0026#34;hello snapshot\\u0026#34;));\\n    BlobSnapshotInfo snapshotInfo = await blob.CreateSnapshotAsync();\\n    string snapshotId = snapshotInfo.Snapshot;\\n    Uri blobUri = blob.Uri;\\n    TokenCredential tokenCredential = new DefaultAzureCredential();\\n    try\\n    {\\n#endif\\n    #region Snippet:DownloadBlobSnapshot\\n    BlobsStorageResourceProvider blobs = new(tokenCredential);\\n    TransferManager transferManager = new TransferManager();\\n\\n    BlockBlobStorageResourceOptions sourceOptions = new BlockBlobStorageResourceOptions()\\n    {\\n        Snapshot = snapshotId\\n    };\\n\\n    TransferOperation transferOperation = await transferManager.StartTransferAsync(\\n        sourceResource: await blobs.FromBlobAsync(blobUri, sourceOptions),\\n        destinationResource: LocalFilesStorageResourceProvider.FromFile(downloadPath));\\n    await transferOperation.WaitForCompletionAsync();\\n    #endregion\\n#if !SNIPPET\\n    }\\n    finally\\n    {\\n        await container.DeleteIfExistsAsync();\\n        if (File.Exists(downloadPath)) File.Delete(downloadPath);\\n    }\\n#endif\\n}\\n\\n[Test]\\n[LiveOnly]\\n[NonParallelizable]\\npublic async Task CopyBlobVersionAsync()\\n{\\n#if !SNIPPET\\n    string connectionString = StorageConnectionString;\\n    string containerName = Randomize(\\u0026#34;sample-container\\u0026#34;);\\n    string srcBlobName = Randomize(\\u0026#34;sample-blob\\u0026#34;);\\n    string dstBlobName = Randomize(\\u0026#34;sample-blob-dest\\u0026#34;);\\n    BlobServiceClient service = new BlobServiceClient(connectionString);\\n    BlobContainerClient container = service.GetBlobContainerClient(containerName);\\n    await container.CreateIfNotExistsAsync();\\n    BlockBlobClient srcBlob = container.GetBlockBlobClient(srcBlobName);\\n    await srcBlob.UploadAsync(BinaryData.FromString(\\u0026#34;version content\\u0026#34;));\\n    // Upload again to create a new version; capture the first version ID from properties\\n    BlobProperties props = await srcBlob.GetPropertiesAsync();\\n    string versionId = props.VersionId;\\n    Uri srcBlobUri = srcBlob.Uri;\\n    Uri dstBlobUri = container.GetBlockBlobClient(dstBlobName).Uri;\\n    TokenCredential tokenCredential = new DefaultAzureCredential();\\n    try\\n    {\\n#endif\\n    #region Snippet:CopyBlobVersion\\n    BlobsStorageResourceProvider blobs = new(tokenCredential);\\n    TransferManager transferManager = new TransferManager();\\n\\n    BlockBlobStorageResourceOptions sourceOptions = new BlockBlobStorageResourceOptions()\\n    {\\n        VersionId = versionId\\n    };\\n\\n    TransferOperation transferOperation = await transferManager.StartTransferAsync(\\n        sourceResource: await blobs.FromBlobAsync(srcBlobUri, sourceOptions),\\n        destinationResource: await blobs.FromBlobAsync(dstBlobUri, new BlockBlobStorageResourceOptions()));\\n    await transferOperation.WaitForCompletionAsync();\\n    #endregion\\n#if !SNIPPET\\n    }\\n    finally\\n    {\\n        await container.DeleteIfExistsAsync();\\n    }\\n#endif\\n}\\n```\\n\\n#### 2b. Add snippet placeholders to the README\\n\\n**File to edit:** `sdk/storage/Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Blobs/README.md`\\n\\nAdd the following section after the existing `### Blob Copy` section (before `### Resume using ShareFilesStorageResourceProvider`):\\n\\n````markdown\\n### Snapshot and Version Transfers\\n\\nThe `Snapshot` and `VersionId` properties on `BlobStorageResourceOptions` allow targeting a specific point-in-time copy of a blob as the transfer source. These options are only valid for the source resource; they cannot be combined.\\n\\nDownload a specific blob snapshot:\\n\\n```C# Snippet:DownloadBlobSnapshot\\n```\\n\\nCopy a specific blob version to a new blob:\\n\\n```C# Snippet:CopyBlobVersion\\n```\\n````\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README already contains all required sections:\\n1. ✅ Getting started (Install the package, Prerequisites, Authenticate the client)\\n2. ✅ Key concepts\\n3. ✅ Examples — **needs the new Snapshot and Version subsections added per Step 2b**\\n4. ✅ Troubleshooting\\n5. ✅ Next steps\\n6. ✅ Contributing\\n\\nNo structural changes needed beyond the new subsection in Examples.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/storage/Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Blobs/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/storage/Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Blobs/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 storage` — injects the new `#region Snippet:` code into the README placeholders; verify the git diff shows the README was updated\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 storage` — no new public types were added, but run to confirm\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/storage/Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Blobs/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; TokenCredential of AddBlobServiceClient(...) is always DefaultAzureCredential\",\"body\":\"### Library name and version\\n\\nMicrosoft.Extensions.Azure 1.11.0\\n\\n### Describe the bug\\n\\nWithin the `BlobClientBuilderExtensions` the method\\n\\n```C#\\npublic static IAzureClientBuilder\\u003cBlobServiceClient, BlobClientOptions\\u003e AddBlobServiceClient\\u003cTBuilder\\u003e(this TBuilder builder, Uri serviceUri, TokenCredential tokenCredential)\\n    where TBuilder : IAzureClientFactoryBuilderWithCredential\\n{\\n    return builder.RegisterClientFactory\\u003cBlobServiceClient, BlobClientOptions\\u003e((options, token) =\\u003e new BlobServiceClient(serviceUri, token, options));\\n}\\n```\\nis not passing down the provided `TokenCredential`.\\n\\n### Expected behavior\\n\\nThe provided `TokenCredential` will be used, maybe like this:\\n\\n```C#\\npublic static IAzureClientBuilder\\u003cBlobServiceClient, BlobClientOptions\\u003e AddBlobServiceClient\\u003cTBuilder\\u003e(this TBuilder builder, Uri serviceUri, TokenCredential tokenCredential)\\n    where TBuilder : IAzureClientFactoryBuilderWithCredential\\n{\\n    return builder.RegisterClientFactory\\u003cBlobServiceClient, BlobClientOptions\\u003e((options, _) =\\u003e new BlobServiceClient(serviceUri, tokenCredential, options));\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### Actual behavior\\n\\nThe `DefaultAzureCredential` is always being used, which leads to unnecessary token requests, that mostly cannot be resolved and even throw exceptions when some configuration is missing.\\n\\n### Reproduction Steps\\n\\nAdd the service to the `IServiceCollection` like this:\\n\\n```C#\\nservices.AddAzureClients(\\n    config =\\u003e\\n    {\\n        config.AddBlobServiceClient(\\n            new Uri(\\\"\\u003curl to the storage account\\u003e\\\"),\\n            new AzureCliCredential());\\n    });\\n```\\n\\n### Environment\\n\\n_No
